skill.name: biomed-outline-generator
skill.description: Generates structured biomedical outlines for review articles, discussion sections, and thesis proposals. Use when a user provides biomedical keywords, results/discussion text, or a proposal title plus background and needs a directly usable academic writing scaffold.
skill.summary: Generates structured biomedical outlines for review articles, discussion sections, and thesis proposals from keywords or results text.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 33 static findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are substring matches on 'MD' within words like 'biomedical' and 'SKILL.md', not actual MD5 usage. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are markdown code fences (```) and inline code backticks in SKILL.md, which is a pure documentation file. The 'hardcoded URL' is a GitHub source attribution link in a markdown blockquote. The 'system reconnaissance' flag is a false positive in audit metadata JSON. The skill contains no executable payload, no network requests, and no dangerous operations. scripts/validate_skill.py is a trivial argparse script. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

content.value_statement: Researchers waste hours structuring biomedical outlines from scratch. This skill generates manuscript-grade outlines for review articles, discussion sections, and thesis proposals from simple keywords or results text.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured review article outlines with abstract, keywords, chapters, and conclusion
Create discussion section outlines from results paragraphs with interpretation blocks and limitations
Build thesis or proposal outlines with background, research plan, timeline, and expected outcomes
Detect input type automatically as review, discussion, or proposal from user context
Apply deterministic section ordering and stable heading labels across repeated runs
Refuse non-biomedical or insufficient inputs with clear guidance on accepted formats
limitations: Cannot generate complete manuscripts — produces outlines only, not full text
Requires biomedical context — does not work for non-biomedical topics
Does not fabricate or look up real citations — users must supply their own references
Output quality depends on input detail — vague inputs produce high-level outlines

use_cases.title: Review Article Planning
use_cases.description: A biomedical researcher provides keywords about tumor microenvironment and macrophage polarization. The skill generates a structured review outline with chapters, subchapters, and a conclusion section.

use_cases.title: Discussion Section Drafting
use_cases.description: A PhD student pastes experimental results from a mouse model study. The skill produces a discussion outline with interpretation blocks, literature integration points, and limitations sections.

use_cases.title: Thesis Proposal Development
use_cases.description: A graduate student provides a proposal title and background on exosomal miRNAs for Alzheimer diagnosis. The skill builds a full proposal outline with research plan, feasibility assessment, and timeline.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Review Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Research direction: tumor microenvironment, macrophage polarization, immune checkpoint resistance. Please generate a review outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have research keywords and want a review article structure

prompt_templates.title: Create a Discussion Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: In our mouse model, anti-PD-1 reduced tumor burden, but the effect was lost after CSF1 overexpression. Please draft a discussion outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have experimental results and need a discussion section outline

prompt_templates.title: Build a Thesis Proposal Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Title: Exosomal miRNAs as early diagnostic biomarkers for Alzheimer disease. Background: include plasma exosomes, qPCR versus small RNA-seq, validation cohort, and neuroinflammation markers.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a proposal concept and need a structured thesis outline

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Generate from Mixed Input
prompt_templates.prompt: I am studying the role of mitochondrial dysfunction in neurodegenerative diseases. Key pathways: mitophagy, ROS signaling. We found increased oxidative stress markers in patient-derived neurons. Please generate an appropriate biomedical outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have mixed research context and want the skill to auto-detect the best outline type

output_examples.input: Research direction: tumor microenvironment, macrophage polarization, immune checkpoint resistance
output_examples.output:
## Macrophage Polarization in the Tumor Microenvironment: Implications for Immune Checkpoint Resistance

## Abstract

## Keywords

## 1. Introduction

## 2. Macrophage Polarization States in Cancer
### 2.1 M1/M2 Classification and Plasticity
### 2.2 Signaling Pathways Driving Polarization

## 3. Tumor Microenvironment Dynamics
### 3.1 Cellular Composition and Crosstalk
### 3.2 Metabolic and Hypoxic Regulation

## 4. Immune Checkpoint Resistance Mechanisms
### 4.1 Macrophage-Mediated Immune Evasion
### 4.2 Therapeutic Strategies Targeting Reprogramming

## 5. Emerging Research Directions

## 6. Conclusion and Outlook

output_examples.input: In our mouse model, anti-PD-1 reduced tumor burden, but the effect was lost after CSF1 overexpression. Please draft a discussion outline.
output_examples.output:
## Summary of Key Findings

## Interpretation
### Anti-PD-1 Response and CSF1-Mediated Resistance
### Mechanistic Implications

## Integration with Published Literature

## Study Limitations

## Future Directions

## Conclusion

best_practices: Provide specific keywords, pathways, or disease context rather than vague topic descriptions
Include your target journal or institutional formatting requirements when known
Review the generated outline and add your own references and data before drafting
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to write a complete manuscript — it only produces outlines
Do not provide non-biomedical topics — the skill will refuse and redirect you
Do not expect the skill to generate real citations or statistical results

faq.question: What types of biomedical outlines can this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates three types: review article outlines, discussion section outlines, and thesis or proposal outlines.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete research paper?
faq.answer: No. It produces structured outlines only. You must write the full manuscript yourself using the outline as a scaffold.

faq.question: Does this skill provide real citations or references?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not fabricate or look up citations. You must supply your own references and data.

faq.question: What input does the skill need to work?
faq.answer: Provide biomedical keywords, a results paragraph, or a proposal title with background context. More detail produces better outlines.

faq.question: How does the skill decide which outline type to generate?
faq.answer: It automatically detects whether your input matches a review, discussion, or proposal pattern based on keywords and structure.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-biomedical topics?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for biomedical writing only. It will refuse non-biomedical requests and explain accepted input formats.
